pi_items.source: Wind direction conversion to compass bearings verified via https://www.campbellsci.com/blog/convert-wind-directions shows 235° is Southwest wind. Task description states 'Wind Direction 235 degrees' confirming metadata value.

Racing Rules of Sailing require start line perpendicular to wind. With wind at 235°, perpendicular bearings are 145° (left) and 325° (right). Metadata calculation: (235 + (-90) + 360) % 360 = 145° confirmed correct via https://hdgyc.org/racing/race-committee-and-course-instructions/guidance-for-setting-a-race-course

GPS coordinates of Cowes, Isle of Wight from https://latitude.to/map/gb/united-kingdom/cities/cowes are 50.7631°N, -1.2977°W to -1.298°W. Task metadata -1.3 is approximately correct, with <0.005° difference.

GPS coordinates of Cowes from https://latitude.to/map/gb/united-kingdom/cities/cowes and https://www.gps-latitude-longitude.com/gps-coordinates-of-cowes-isle-of-wight are 50.7631°N. Task metadata 50.775°N is approximately 0.012° or 0.72 nautical miles north of actual Cowes location.

Search of racing standards (https://www.racingrulesofsailing.org and https://forums.sailinganarchy.com) found no specific 0.25 nm standard. Cowes start lines are typically ~1 nautical mile per https://purelatitude.com/boat-club-events/cowes-week/. However, 0.25 nm is not contradicted and could be valid for smaller fleets or simplified simulations.
No authoritative source found specifying this tolerance value. Typical racing start line bearing tolerances vary by race management, but 3.0 degrees is reasonable for line setup precision.
No authoritative source found specifying this tolerance value. 10% distance tolerance is reasonable for start line buoy placement verification in racing scenarios.
No authoritative source found. 0.001 degree tolerance (~0.06 nautical miles) appears reasonable for GPS coordinate precision verification in navigation tasks.

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic sailing scenario set in the Solent near Cowes, Isle of Wight. Verified facts: (1) Target wind direction 235° is correctly specified as Southwest wind, consistent with sailing conditions. (2) Target bearing offset of -90° is correct for perpendicular start line placement per Racing Rules of Sailing, placing the pin end at 145° bearing. (3) Coordinates are approximately correct for Cowes area (-1.3°W verified, latitude 50.775°N is ~0.72 nm north of actual Cowes at 50.763°N). (4) Start line distance of 0.25 nautical miles is shorter than typical Cowes lines (~1 nm) but is not contradicted by any standard and may be intentional for the simulation. (5) All tolerance values are reasonable but not based on authoritative standards. Bridge Command is a real open-source ship simulator from GitHub used as the execution environment.
